task: Review one no-write LLM Editor planning brief for site fit, risk, and readiness.
prompt: Act as the LLM Reviewer gate for lastzguides.com. Review the planning brief only. Do not write or rewrite public page copy. Do not create patch specs. Check duplicate intent, cluster role separation, canonical claim protection, template safety, owner questions, draft exact replacement safety, and deterministic QA readiness. Treat analytics as signals, not proof. If the Editor includes exact_replacements, review whether they are narrow, target-only, literal exact_old/exact_new candidates with owner_approval_required=true. Do not approve apply_preview from an LLM-only exact replacement. Use plain ASCII English only. Return JSON only.

selected_opportunity.rationale: This is a plausible progression support update if the reference can be verified against other reliable sources or owner confirmation. It aligns with an existing HQ page and has a distinct planning job.
selected_opportunity.player_value: Players can verify HQ requirements and construction dependencies before making progression mistakes.
selected_opportunity.duplication_risk: Medium because HQ coverage may already exist elsewhere in the cluster and needs intent checking.
selected_opportunity.next_step: Human review should confirm the external claim, then determine whether this is a scope-safe update to the existing HQ page.
claims_to_verify: The actual HQ requirement and dependency structure.
Whether the external reference matches canonical game knowledge.
Whether the page already covers this planning job adequately.

deterministic_proposal.title: External source opportunity: HQ and progression requirement cross-check
evidence: Owner-approved wiki/reference source can reveal HQ and progression planning gaps.
External source URL recorded for later manual verification.

site_fit.primary_user_job: Verify HQ requirement planning, construction dependencies, and progression-route coverage against reference data.
site_fit.archetype_reason: External source indicates a possible player job to validate.

constraints: Use this as topic discovery and cross-validation context only.
Verify public claims against canonical site memory and at least one additional reliable source or owner confirmation.
reject_if: The topic duplicates an existing page intent without adding a distinct player job.
The external claim cannot be verified beyond this source.
The proposal would blur existing cluster roles.

deterministic_editor_context.primary_query_family: External source opportunity: HQ and progression requirement cross-check
deterministic_editor_context.primary_user_job: Verify HQ requirement planning, construction dependencies, and progression-route coverage against reference data.
deterministic_editor_context.first_screen_answer: Preserve the existing answer-first shape, but make the opening answer clearly satisfy `External source opportunity: HQ and progression requirement cross-check` without changing `hq.html` into a different page role.
do_not_change: Do not publish or apply content changes from this brief automatically.
Do not change the existing page template, navigation pattern, or schema family without separate approval.
Do not create a new page when the Scout proposal recommends updating an existing page.
Do not use analytics signals as proof that a rewrite is required.
The topic duplicates an existing page intent without adding a distinct player job.
The external claim cannot be verified beyond this source.
The proposal would blur existing cluster roles.

HQ Upgrade Requirements: What Buildings Actually Matter

llm_editor_brief.brief_summary: Keep hq.html as a cornerstone progression guide, but tighten the first-screen answer so it explicitly cross-checks HQ requirements and dependency planning without changing page role or cluster fit.
llm_editor_brief.primary_user_job: Verify HQ requirement planning, construction dependencies, and progression-route coverage against reference data.
llm_editor_brief.first_screen_plan: Preserve the answer-first structure. Keep the opening focused on the best HQ path for most players, but make the first visible answer clearly state the requirement cross-check angle: early rush, required-buildings-only upgrades, HQ30 target, then a separate HQ31-35 steel phase. Avoid adding a new intent or expanding into a different guide type. Keep the short supporting line immediately after the answer so users can confirm this is a planning guide, not a full route map.

section_plan.action: Keep the existing answer-first block, but make the promise more explicit about HQ requirement verification and progression planning.
section_plan.reason: This is the highest-value first-screen area and should immediately satisfy the cross-check intent.

section_plan.action: Add or refine the summary logic around when to rush, when to stop for required buildings, and when HQ30 becomes the main checkpoint.
section_plan.reason: Users need a simple decision rule, not just a level list.

section_plan.action: Clarify the difference between normal early progression and the heavier HQ31-35 phase, including why steel changes the plan.
section_plan.reason: This keeps the guide useful for planning and reduces confusion about the late-game jump.

section_plan.action: Keep the route block limited to the existing progression cluster and make the internal route order explicit.
section_plan.reason: The page should reinforce the existing cluster role and avoid drifting into unrelated topics.

section_plan.action: Retain related links, but prioritize pages that support progression, leveling, building order, and resource planning.
section_plan.reason: This helps users move to adjacent planning pages without broadening the page intent.

internal_link_plan.reason: Keep the main hub link as the top-level route into the progression cluster.
Supports new players who need the earliest progression context before HQ planning.
Helps users optimize the pre-HQ30 phase without duplicating HQ-specific coverage.
Directly supports dependency and building-priority planning.
Useful follow-up for users who want detailed HQ cost and requirement data.
Supports resource planning around upgrade timing and bottlenecks.
Relevant for the HQ31-35 progression phase where steel becomes the limiter.

do_not_change: Do not change the existing page template, navigation pattern, or schema family.
Do not turn hq.html into a different page role or a new intent.
Do not add unsupported external game claims beyond the current canonical framing.
Do not use analytics as proof of a rewrite need.
Do not broaden the page into a competitor-style reference dump.
Do not create a new page for this opportunity.
owner_questions: Can the external HQ and progression reference be confirmed against canonical site memory or owner-approved reference data before any user-visible wording is finalized?
Do you want the first-screen answer to mention HQ30 and HQ31-35 explicitly, or keep the current phrasing and only sharpen the supporting sentence?
Should the cluster route block stay exactly as-is structurally, with only wording tightened for route clarity?

exact_replacements.exact_old: Best Last Z HQ upgrade path for most players: rush early levels, upgrade only required buildings, push efficiently to HQ30 first, and treat HQ31-35 as a separate steel-based progression phase with much heavier timers.
exact_replacements.exact_new: Best Last Z HQ upgrade path for most players: rush early levels, upgrade only the buildings required for the next HQ checkpoint, push efficiently to HQ30 first, and treat HQ31-35 as a separate steel-based progression phase with much heavier timers.
exact_replacements.reason: This is a narrow first-screen refinement that preserves the current structure while improving requirement clarity.

llm_editor_brief.next_step: Have an owner verify the external reference against canonical knowledge, then decide whether this can be applied as a narrow first-screen clarification with no page-role change.

guardrails: No content files may be edited by Reviewer.
No final public page copy may be generated.
No patch specs, backlog entries, manifests, PRs, or production changes may be created.
Editor exact_replacements are proposal-only candidates; they do not approve copy or bypass propose/approval/apply-approved.
If exact_replacements are present, owner_approval_required must remain true.
High-risk cornerstone/home pages cannot be advanced without explicit owner review.
Owner approval is required before any user-visible content change.
Use plain ASCII English only in every string field.

exact_replacement_review.description: Review of any Editor exact_replacements. Empty-array Editor briefs still need a short no-candidate note.
